prerequisites: An RD Station Marketing account with permission to create applications in the RD Station App Store.

An application registered in the RD Station App Store via the App Publisher (https://appstore.rdstation.com/pt-BR/publisher), with at least one callback (redirect) URL configured. This yields the client_id and client_secret.
An OAuth 2.0 refresh_token obtained by completing the authorization-code flow against the registered application.
A start_date value, used as the lower bound from which to begin syncing data.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 (authorization code)
authMethods.summary: RD Station Marketing's API uses OAuth 2.0. You register an application to receive a client_id and client_secret, complete the authorization-code flow once to obtain a refresh_token, and supply all three (authorization.client_id, authorization.client_secret, authorization.refresh_token) to the connector. The connector uses the refresh_token to mint short-lived access_tokens (each access_token is valid for 86400 seconds / 24 hours) against the token endpoint https://api.rd.services/auth/token.

setupSteps.title: Create an application and get client_id and client_secret
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the RD Station App Publisher at https://appstore.rdstation.com/pt-BR/publisher with your RD Station account. Choose 'Quero criar um aplicativo', name the app, and (for internal use) select 'Aplicativo privado'. Register at least one callback (redirect) URL the app will respond to. After creating the app, locate its client_id, client_secret, and callback URL in the app's management page. Apps are private by default; making one public requires submitting it for approval.

setupSteps.title: Authorize the app and obtain a one-time code
setupSteps.body: Direct a browser to the authorization URL https://api.rd.services/auth/dialog?client_id=YOUR_CLIENT_ID&redirect_uri=YOUR_CALLBACK_URL (optionally with a state parameter). The redirect_uri must exactly match a callback URL registered on the app. After granting access, RD Station redirects to your callback URL with a code parameter. This code is valid for 1 hour.

setupSteps.title: Exchange the code for tokens
setupSteps.body: Send POST https://api.rd.services/auth/token?token_by=code with a JSON body containing client_id, client_secret, and the code. The response returns an access_token (valid 86400 seconds / 24 hours) and a refresh_token. Save the refresh_token; the code is no longer needed after this step. If the code has expired you receive the error EXPIRED_CODE_GRANT and must repeat the authorization step.

setupSteps.title: Provide credentials and start_date to the connector
setupSteps.body: Configure the connector's authorization fields with the client_id (authorization.client_id), client_secret (authorization.client_secret), and refresh_token (authorization.refresh_token). Set the required start_date config field to the earliest date from which data should be synced. The connector renews the access_token automatically by POSTing client_id, client_secret, and refresh_token to https://api.rd.services/auth/token.

sources.title: RD Station Developers - Passo 1: Criar aplicativo na App Store e gerar credenciais
RD Station Developers - Passo 2: Como utilizar as credenciais para gerar o code
RD Station Developers - Passo 3: Como obter os tokens (access_token e refresh_token)
RD Station Developers - Passo 4: Como utilizar o refresh_token para atualizar o access_token
